grammarNotes.body: 以 functions as a preposition "by means of/through." The construction V之以N (e.g., 經之以五事, 校之以計) means "to measure/compare it by means of N." The object 之 precedes 以, a common word-order pattern in Classical Chinese where the object is fronted for emphasis before the preposition. Modern paraphrase: "measure them through the five categories."

The pattern N曰X introduces a definition or categorization: "N is called X." Here 一曰道 "one is called the Way," 二曰天 "two is called Heaven," and so on. This N曰 structure commonly enumerates terms in classical Chinese didactic texts. The 曰 here does not mean "to say" but "is called/is designated."

而 links the parallel verbal phrases 經之以五事, 校之以計 and 索其情, indicating sequential or additive action: "measure... compare... and seek." Unlike English "and," 而 often carries a slightly adversative or emphatic force in Classical Chinese; here it simply coordinates the three investigative steps Sunzi prescribes.
